data.name: Welcome to Grim Hollow
entries: This Grim Hollow adventure is set in the world of Etharis, a place of darkness and terrible evil, where goodness is rare and beautiful.

In this dark fantasy setting from Ghostfire Gaming, most heroes aren't paragons of justice; they're mortals with foibles and flaws. In turn, few villains are avatars of pure evil, hell-bent on destruction.

Dark fantasy stories rarely culminate in wholly happy endings. Innocent lives are lost just as frequently as evil ones, and people must sacrifice all they hold dear to achieve their ends. The choices the characters must make have no righteous answer—only the lesser of two evils.

entries.name: A Valikan Adventure
entries: This adventure takes place in the small Valikan village of Hverfa. It lies in the far north, at the frozen edge of Etharis. It is a harsh land of short summers and frigid winters, where the weak northern sun can fail to rise above the horizon.

The dangers of this land are both natural and supernatural. Its people have a hardened tenacity that is steeped in traditions of violence, not just to survive but also as a religious necessity to forestall the end of the world.
The lands of Etharis are described in the {@book Grim Hollow Campaign Guide|GrimHollowCG24|7} and {@book Grim Hollow Player's Guide|GrimHollowPG24|8|The Valikan Clans}.

entries.name: Winter's Breath
entries: Winter's Breath is a four-hour, one-shot 5e adventure set in Hverfa, a village on the eastern shores of the frozen lands of Valika. It is optimized for six 3rd-level characters, who are asked by the jarl of the village to deal with a monstrous threat.

entries.name: Playing This Adventure
entries: To play, you need either the free Fifth Edition Basic Rules or the books that make up the Fifth Edition core rules.

entries.name: Premade Characters
entries: To help you get started playing this adventure as quickly as possible, Ghostfire Gaming has created a set of ready-to-use player characters for this adventure, which can be found here:

entries.name: Monsters, Magic, and More
entries: When you see text in {@b Bold}, that's usually the adventure's way of telling you a scene or location contains a monster or NPC with a stat block that's ready to fight. New monsters and NPCs are found in Appendix A and are identified with an asterisk (\*). If a monster's name appears in bold and isn't followed by an asterisk, then that monster's game statistics are found in the Fifth Edition rules.

When you see text in Italics, it often indicates that you're looking at the name of a spell (like {@spell Tasha's Hideous Laughter|XPHB|Hideous Laughter}) or a magic item (like {@item Winged Boots|XDMG}). Most spells and magic items are found in the Fifth Edition rules. If new spells or magic items are included in the adventure, they appear in an appendix at the end of the adventure.
If you read text that is in Small Caps, it refers to other encounters or sections of the adventure (such as "Winter Wolf Attack").

When you see boxed text like this, the characters have likely entered a new area or triggered an event, such as an NPC entering the scene. This text is meant for the GM to read or paraphrase aloud to the players to set the scene.

entries.name: Themes
entries: In the far north, at the frozen edge of Etharis, spreads the home of the Valikan clans. It is a harsh land of short summers and frigid winters where the weak northern sun can fail to rise above the horizon.

The dangers of this land are both natural and supernatural. Its people have a hardened tenacity that is steeped in traditions of violence, not just to survive but also as a religious necessity to forestall the end of the world.

In Etharis, many seemingly benevolent people hide an inner darkness. This adventure explores what people are willing to do to gain power and hold on to it, even in the face of others' suffering. The characters may uncover truths that force them to reevaluate their loyalties and decide how justice is best served.

data.name: Introduction
entries: {@i The following sections provide an introduction to the geographic setting and an overview of the adventure.}

entries.name: Welcome to Hverfa
entries: Hverfa is a modest but wealthy village on Valika's eastern coast. The settlement is nestled within a fjord that abuts the Shadow Storm Gulf. Its hinterlands are shrouded in the gloom of the forest known as Skuggiskogr. Though isolated, Hverfa has frequent visitors. Clan raiders stop here on their journey south to raid Ostoya in the summer, and the when traveling north again to return home for autumn.

Hverfa is aligned with the great Clan Morgöng. The berserkers from Hverfa are renowned for their ferocity. Their ruthless violence is a distraction from the more cunning machinations of their clan. Yet Hverfa's number of warriors always remains small. Anyone who gains enough fame through raiding is invariably enticed to join larger settlements.

Nevertheless, Hverfa's raiding success has decorated their longhouse in gold. Always gold and never silver. Jarl Bödvar Örskýrsson is an older man now, yet he has personally trained and led the berserkers of Hverfa for decades. Alongside his riches, Jarl Örskýrsson has gathered a circle of loyal warriors.

entries.name: Adventure Background
entries: Jarl Bödvar Örskýrsson became jarl of Hverfa because his older brother Sigurð died inside the Skuggiskogr forest. Bödvar told the village that his brother died while the pair hunted for wolf pelts. The pack surrounded the brothers, and Sigurð gave his life so Bödvar could escape.

The truth is much darker. The brothers met the fey forest queen Svár beneath the dark boughs of Skuggiskogr. The fey offered them strength beyond reckoning, and Bödvar accepted. Svár taught him the Lunar Sacrament: a ritual to give one the curse of lycanthropy. To complete the ritual, Bödvar had to murder his brother and eat his heart. Thus, the second son of Örksýr av Hverfa inherited his father's position when the old jarl died.

Svár's offer came with conditions. After Bödvar's thirtieth winter as jarl, he would have to enter Skuggiskogr and submit himself to serve as a champion of Svár's faerie court. Bödvar's thirtieth winter has come and passed. The jarl has not submitted to the forest queen. Therefore, Hverfa is about to suffer the consequences of the jarl's dishonor.

That suffering takes the form of a monstrous white wolf whose breath holds the power of coldfire. Winter wolves are rare monstrosities that only appear in remote wildernesses or when summoned by the fey courts. They are a symbol of doom and reckoning. The monster is only satisfied once it has eaten the Jarl.

Jarl Örskýrsson sent warriors into the forest to slay the monster, but none have returned. Knowing the likely truth of the situation, the jarl is too frightened to confront the {@creature Winter Wolf|XMM} himself. He fears being eaten or having his secret revealed.

entries.name: Adventure Summary
entries: The breaking of an ancient fey's boon threatens the inhabitants of a remote village.

entries.name: Part One: Hverfa
entries: The characters are asked to investigate a monster troubling the village of Hverfa. The monster has emerged from the Skuggiskogr, but no one is entirely sure what it is. The can learn more about the threat by speaking with folk in the village longhouse, with a seer gnome named Harbard, and at the homestead of Nora Seaview, the location of the most recent attack.

entries.name: Part Two: Exploring Skuggiskogr
entries: In the Skuggiskogr, the characters find several trails.

One leads to Hverfa warriors slaughtered by the Winter Wolf. Nearby, a lone surivor, T'vir av Setstaðr, lies on the brink of death and threatened by the chilled dead drawn to her presence. If rescued, T'vir may present a boon to fight the {@creature Winter Wolf|XMM} if she thinks the characters are worthy.

Another trail leads to a confrontation with the massive white wolf and its pack. Should the {@creature Winter Wolf|XMM} escape, it flees into the woods to warn its mistress. The characters can follow the beast to a glade where the fey queen Svár waits and possibly learn of Jarl Örskýrsson's secret.

entries.name: Part Three: Justice or Judgement
entries: If the party learns Örskýrsson's secret, they must choose whether to aid the jarl in dealing with the {@creature Winter Wolf|XMM} or aid Svár in seeking her revenge.

entries.name: Gold
entries: During the adventure, the characters have the opportunity to earn the following gold rewards:

entries.name: Adventure Hooks
entries: The following hooks can draw the characters into the adventure. The players may also wish to invent their own reasons for helping Hverfa.

entries.name: We Survive Together
entries: You are part of Hverfa's community. You may have grown up in the village, settled here after a period of travel, or arrived as a thrall from distant lands. Someone close to you may have already been slain by the monster or were among the jarl's warriors sent to kill it. Or maybe you simply wish to slay the beast before further harm is caused.

entries.name: Traveling Monster Slayer
entries: You are a traveling adventurer hired by the jarl to hunt the monster. You may have been in Hverfa when the attacks began or traveled here from nearby after hearing of the trouble. Your motivations may be purely monetary, an honorable desire to help a community in need, or curiosity about the monsters living in the Skuggiskogr.

entries.name: For the Clan!
entries: You have been sent by Clan Morgöng to alleviate the troubles in Hverfa. The clan cannot afford an asset like Hverfa to be destroyed by monsters. You have no special loyalty to Jarl Örskýrsson; your mission is simply to ensure the long-term survival of the settlement by any means.

entries.name: Important NPCs
entries: Below is a brief summary of the most important NPCs during this adventure, including tips on how to roleplay them.

entries.name: Jarl Bödvar Örskýrsson
entries: {@i (YARL BAHD-vahr ohrs-KEER-suhn)}
{@i Lawful evil, male human Werebear (see appendix A)}

Jarl Örskýrsson is respected in Hverfa. He has personally led successful raids every summer for the past thirty years, and the stories of his strength and ferocity in battle have been told and retold over the autumn bonfires. The jarl personally trains the warriors of Hverfa. His inner guard are mighty berserkers, loyal to him and willing to die in his defense. He is beloved by his people without question.

Those who Jarl Örskýrsson can't sway with his reputation, he instead tries to intimidate. His stoic and gruff exterior hides the insecurities of a man who does not believe he deserves his position. The jarl is proud and powerful, but his ego is brittle—especially when the truth about his brother's fate is in question. The jarl is easily provoked to violence by those who displease him or question his leadership and prowess. Because of this, he is feared even by those loyal to him.

entries.name: Harbard, Brother Gruel
entries: {@i Lawful good, male forest gnome}

Harbard is a respected elder in Hverfa and a known seer. His nickname comes from his habit of preparing hot bowls of porridge for visitors who seek his wisdom and guidance. The fare is nourishing but tastes terrible.

Harbard was a contemporary of Jarl Örskýr av Hverfa, the current jarl's father. He knew the brothers Bödvar and Sigurð when they were boys, and he makes no secret that he believes Sigurð was the better leader for Hverfa. Though he has dark visions of Sigurð's fate, something clouds his ability to accurately divine events that occur within the Skuggiskogr.
Harbard has a gregarious nature and a deep booming voice for a man of his gnomish stature. When he augurs futures, he speaks as if telling some great saga.

entries.name: Nora Seaview
entries: {@i Lawful Good, female elf}

Nora lives a quiet life in a cottage at the top of the fjord a small distance from Hverfa's center. When she was young, she hunted game and foraged berries inside the Skuggiskogr. Now that she's older and Jarl Örskýrsson has forbidden exploration of the woods, she weaves tapestries using the wool of her small flock of goats.
Nora has a good heart but is as blunt as most who've grown up in the harsh Valikan climate. She rarely smiles. She says only what is necessary. She is kind but frozen by an old sadness.

entries.name: T'vir av Setstaðr
entries: {@i (tuh-VEER set-STAH-duhr)}
{@i Chaotic neutral, female human Veteran}

T'vir is a young warrior who grew up in Hverfa, earning herself the name "T'vir of Flamebourne" for wielding her family's ancestral sword. The weapon is inlaid with a powerful flame rune. T'vir is respected for her leadership and strength in battle, and though she is loyal to the jarl, many in Hverfa prefer her to Örskýrsson.

T'vir is proud and stubborn. She angers quickly because she has learned from Jarl Örskýrsson that rage is a weapon. This makes her spiteful to her enemies, especially when she believes she has been betrayed. Yet T'vir is also very loyal to those who win her trust. Honoring her by returning T'vir's family sword, which she gave to the heroes to protect them from the {@creature Winter Wolf|XMM}, secures her friendship for life.

entries.name: Raatvidur
entries: {@i (RAHT-vid-uhr)}
{@i Neutral evil, nongendered puck (fey)}

Raatvidur was once a dryad that cared for certain trees in the Skuggiskogr, but the corrupting magic of the Winter fey court changed it into a puck. It now travels the forest looking to grow new trees in ground infused with the corpses of fleshy creatures. It wants its former domain back, but Svár now rules this part of the forest instead. It is curious and talkative in a horrifying way.

entries.name: Svár
entries: {@i (suh-VAHR)}
{@i Chaotic evil, norn (fey)}

Svár is an ancient fey queen of the winter court. Her domain once stretched much further than the Skuggiskogr. She has, at different times, been an aid and an adversary to the Valikans making their homes on the forest's edge. Though her whims seem capricious to the clanfolk who know her (usually by different names in different villages), earning her favor usually depends on whether she feels respected and appreciated.

As a norn, Svár is a weaver of fates. She provides gifts that allow her beneficiaries to seize a bright destiny, though she never offers a thing without expecting recompense. Though her wrath can be overbearing when she feels betrayed, Svár is not cruel—she's simply contractual. She believes any suffering experienced by mortals is the result of their own choices, never her undue malice.

data.name: Part 1: Hverfa
entries: The characters have, through whichever means makes the most sense, been called to the longhouse of the village of Hverfa. They are currently standing outside the longhouse, waiting to be called in to speak with the leader of the village: Jarl Bödvar Örskýrsson.

items: Ask the players to introduce their characters to each other if they haven't done so already.

If the player-characters are from Hverfa, ask them about their place in the community. What job or role do they fulfill that contributes to Hverfa's survival? Are they close with the jarl or any other village NPCs?

Ask any player whose character is not from the village to describe why they're visiting. Are they leaving or arriving in Valika now that the winter snows are melting? Are they Valikan themselves or travelers from distant lands?
Ask each player to describe why they have answered the jarl's summons. Do they wish to help the community, or do they seek payment? Are they here for glory, or does another motive bring them?

entries.name: Hverfa Longhouse
entries: The longhouse is a surprisingly impressive building for a village the size of Hverfa. It's built mostly from stone, though its roof is the retired hull of a Valikan longship.

The longhouse is very warm compared to the brisk Valikan air outside. The large common hall is lit by burning torches and a roaring central firepit. The firelight glitters off the many gold trinkets decorating the hall. Gold has even been inlaid into carvings on the support beams, and the walls are hung with tapestries telling many stories of past raiding triumphs.

Jarl Örskýrsson sits in his highbacked throne of ivory and fur, raised on a rostrum that overlooks the hall. He is accompanied by a retinue of berserkers: his inner circle. The hall is crowded with interested or concerned villagers.
Once they're assembled, Jarl Örskýrsson speaks with the party. Use the bullet points to guide the conversation.

items: A dangerous monster has emerged from the Skuggiskogr, an untamed and massive forest near Hverfa. This creature attacked homes and farmsteads on the outskirts of the village. Folks have also disappeared on the roads between homesteads.
The identity of the monster isn't known, but it's an incredibly large animal, judging by prints left in the melting winter snow. It's possibly a dire bear.
Nobody is known to have survived an encounter with the monster, and therefore there are no eyewitness accounts of it.
Nora Seaview, who lives on the edge of the village, claims the monster ate her livestock and tried to break into her homestead. She may have useful information (see "Nora Seaview").

Almost a week ago, a warband of over twenty warriors was sent to the forest to track and slay the monster. They were led by T'vir av Setstaðr, one of the town's most capable warriors. It is very concerning that neither she nor any of her groups have returned.
The jarl offers the party either 100 gold coins each, or a boon within his power to grant if the party return with the monster's head.
If she has been slain, recovering T'vir av Setstaðr's magical sword also earns a reward from the jarl—unless the party wishes to keep it.

entries: After the party has conversed with the jarl and the meeting at the longhouse is ending, a villager named Halga approaches the party. She provides a bit more information that the party might find useful:

items: Harbard, a gnome who all the villagers call "Brother Gruel," lives within the village. He is a seer, and he might have a different take on what is happening here, given that he was friends with the previous jarl (see "Harbard, Brother Gruel").
Jarl Örskýrsson has a distaste for the seer, but Harbard might be able to augur useful information using his second sight.

Jarl Örskýrsson has been an honorable and unflagging leader for thirty years. Halga would never say a bad word about the jarl, but some in the village believe T'vir av Setstaðr would make a good leader when the jarl passes on. Her disappearance into the forest has many residents mourning the loss of one of their most cherished citizens.

entries: Once the party are satisfied that they have enough information, they can continue.

entries.name: Harbard, Brother Gruel
entries: Harbard's small cottage is among the cluster of homes built near the center of Hverfa.

Smoke plumes from the chimney of an undersized cottage. The inside smells of salt and herbs, and everything is sized for a gnome, making it very cozy for folks of larger stature. Plenty of cushions, low chairs, and furs are available to nestle upon. A great cauldron bubbles with porridge over the open fireplace, a wooden ladle leaned at the ready against it.

Harbard is friendly to visitors and welcomes them inside for a bowl of gruel. He is happy to answer questions, especially if the characters seem willing to help the village fend off the monster from the Skuggiskogr. However, before talking, the gnome insists the characters have a bowl of gruel.

The stuff tastes awful. Anyone taking even a spoonful must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw to keep from spitting it out. Even if they succeed on that, it requires a DC 10 Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB}) check if the character tries to pretend it is palatable. Anyone who can succeed on both checks earns the admiration and favor of Brother Gruel. He also insists such characters take a helping of the gruel with them on their journey into the forest. This sealed bowl of gruel is {@item Porridge of Healing|GrimHollowWB}, which acts as a {@item Potion of Healing|XPHB}.
From Harbard, the party can learn the following:

items: The Skuggiskogr is a dark place, where many folks from Hverfa have lost their lives, or been terribly injured, over the years. Even entering the edges of the forest can be dangerous, for it is the realm of powerful fey, vicious beasts, carnivorous plants, and untamed elementals. Harbard finds it impossible to augur or divine locations or events occurring beneath the forest's dark canopy.

For this reason, the Skuggiskogr is poorly mapped, and few in Hverfa know it especially well. The jarl has forbidden expeditions inside the forest since his brother's death there, over thirty years prior.

Harbard is familiar with the story of Jarl Örskýrsson and his brother Sigurð. Both went inside the forest to hunt for wolf pelts, but only Bödvar emerged a few days later. Harbard finds the story of a wolf attack suspicious, considering Bödvar didn't have a scratch on him when he emerged.
Harbard suspects Jarl Örskýrsson's story is a lie. He's never been able to prove it, however, even with his divination talents.

Despite his suspicions, Jarl Örskýrsson has proven to be an able leader. He has brought great honor and prosperity to Hverfa. The next important raid into the land of Ostoya is only a few days away, and it is vital that this raid be prepared for and carried out properly.
Harbard can offer little help about the monster attacking the village, except to say he doubts it's a mere bear or similar beast. He can't divine it, meaning it is being protected by powerful magic.

entries: If Harbard likes the party (i.e. someone really "enjoyed" his gruel), he can cast the {@spell Augury|XPHB} spell using a bowl of gruel for the material component. He can provide this service once per day. If Harbard doesn't like the party, he only casts the spell if he is paid 25 gold coins. Note that this divination cannot penetrate the Skuggiskogr or focus on the {@creature Winter Wolf|XMM}, but otherwise the spell can succeed.

entries.name: Nora Seaview
entries: Nora's small homestead sits atop the fjord, near the edge of the forest.

The thatched roof of Nora Seaview's humble stone cottage is half collapsed on one side. Stone bricks have tumbled down next to a damaged wall. A tarp haphazardly covers the exposed areas. The rest of the house is covered by melting snow, and the ground around it is turned to mud. The adjoining paddock is teeming with the scattered corpses of dismembered goats.

As the characters approach, Nora emerges around the side of the house at a sprint, screaming for help. She is chased by 4 {@creature Dire Wolf|XMM|Dire Wolves}, who were drawn to the homestead by the smell of the goats' blood.
The characters can intervene, save Nora from the beasts, and then earn the reward of her information. If they do, Nora tells the story of the monster's attack from the previous week.

items: She was inside her home weaving a tapestry when she thought a sudden storm must have rolled in from the ocean. She heard her goats bleating and running about the yard, but when she peered out a window it was too dark for her to see anything.
Suddenly her roof cracked and almost caved in. She thought it had been struck by a sudden wind.
Her house became so cold that the windows frosted over and the fire died to embers. The weather was not cold enough to do such a thing.

Then she heard a growling so deep it sounded like rattling thunder. Nora didn't dare leave her home until the next day. She found her goats had been slaughtered and her yard and home covered with fresh frost.

entries: Nora is happy to tell her story and withholds no details unless she is interrupted. She told the same story to T'vir av Setstaðr days prior, before T'vir lead her warriors into the forest.

entries.name: Investigating the Yard
entries: If the party chooses to investigate Nora's yard, there are several clues they can discover about the monster that attacked.

A character looking for tracks can spot them in the mud and melting snow with a successful DC 10 Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) check. They are massive paw prints that clearly don't belong to a bear or a drake. They can be easily followed from Nora's homestead into the Skuggiskogr.

A character that succeeds a DC 15 Intelligence ({@skill Nature|XPHB}) check deduces that the snowfall that Nora experienced seems isolated to her home and yard. The monster must have caused it unnaturally.

A character that investigates the remains of Nora's goats and succeeds a DC 10 Wisdom ({@skill Medicine|XPHB}) check notices that they've been ripped to shreds by massive claws. Their entrails are scattered across the yard, requiring great strength. It appears some have been partially or fully eaten.

After gathering at least one clue, a character that wishes to deduce what monster the party are hunting can make a DC 15 Intelligence ({@skill Nature|XPHB}) check. This check has Advantage if more than one clue was gathered. A successful check learns the creature is clearly Large-sized monstrosity. It either has little intelligence as it failed to break into Nora's home, or it ate its fill of her goats. If the check is successful by 5 or more, the character learns the monster is a {@creature Winter Wolf|XMM}.
At this point, the characters can easily follow the trail of the winter wolf and the Valikan warriors into the forest. Continue with Part 2.

data.name: Part 2: Exploring Skuggiskogr
entries: After gaining information in Part 1, the characters are ready to brave the dark forest known as the Skuggiskogr in search of the terrible beast that has been terrorizing the region around Hverfa. Here, they can find T'vir and the remains of her hunting party, and they may learn the true story of how the jarl came to gain such power and fame in his lifetime.

entries.name: On the Beast's Trail
entries: From beneath the eaves of Skuggiskogr is a breathtaking view of the fjord, and of Hverfa far below.

The thick forest canopy only allows dim light to filter through onto the forest floor, and a misty shroud makes seeing past twenty feet impossible. There's little undergrowth except for patches of stubborn grass and bluish moss half-covered by receding snow. The cold air of winter lingers beneath the boughs, where the sun of spring has difficulty reaching. The ground is uneven and difficult to navigate thanks to the large snaking roots of the great trees.

The Winter Wolf is easy to track (no check needed) after entering the forest. It leaves behind large pawprints in the melting snow, which are easy to follow and clearly don't belong to a bear or a drake.

At any time, a character that wishes to deduce what type of monster the tracks belong to can make a DC 13 Intelligence ({@skill Nature|XPHB}) check. A successful learns the prints are from a Large-sized wolf. The creature is clearly a predator and nothing that nature would produce on its own. If the check is successful by 5 or more, the character learns the monster is a Winter Wolf.

A closer inspection of the ground with a successful DC 13 Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) check also discovers the footprints of T'vir av Setstaðr's warband. The monster has left more recent tracks than T'vir's warband, and the two trails lead in different directions. If the characters choose to follow the Winter Wolf tracks, skip to "Winter Wolf Attack". If the characters instead choose to follow the footprints of T'vir's warband, they go to "Warrior Massacre."

entries.name: Warrior Massacre
entries: The characters discover the location where T'vir av Setstaðr's warband met the monster.

The woods are spattered with the blood of massacred Valikans. The fallen warriors lie in pieces, their entrails draped from branches and scattered across tree roots. Their remains have turned purple and blue with frost. The violent scene suggests their enemy was a heartless monster. Runes upon a large tree near the center of the massacre are painted using the warriors' blood.
The runes upon the tree are Valiki and read simply: "Pay Your Debt." The large footprints of the winter wolf are easily noticed among the slaughter and can be followed deeper into the woods.

Inspecting the remains of the Hverfa warriors with a successful DC 13 Wisdom ({@skill Medicine|XPHB}) check notices some of them have had their skin turn blue and their fingertips turn black. They were freezing to death in addition to their other injuries. The springtime weather isn't cold enough for anyone to have frozen in this manner.

Footprints and a trail of blood leading away from the massacre can be spotted by any creature with a passive Perception score of 13 or higher or spotted with a successful DC 13 Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) check.
If the characters choose to follow the wolf tracks, skip to "Winter Wolf Attack." If the characters instead choose to follow the blood trail, go to "T'vir's Rest."

entries.name: T'vir's Rest
entries: Following the blood trail doesn't lead the characters far from the scene of the massacre. T'vir av Setstaðr (Veteran) leans against a tree, attempting to fend off a group of undead creatures.

A woman in Valikan warrior's garb stands with her back against a tree. One arm hangs limp, black from frostbite. The other arm is tucked tight against her body where blood flows from a grievous wound, but is fending off a number of frozen undead creatures with sword wreathed in flames. Blood is frozen to her armor. She is pale and weakened but putting up a valiant fight.

T'vir av Setstaðr is weak from lack of sleep, exposure, and her injuries. She has a 10 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} remaining and two {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels. A group of 5 {@creature Rime Hunger|GrimHollowWB|Rime Hungers}* rose from the corpses of her dead comrades recently, and they are hungry for her warmth and life force.

The {@creature Rime Hunger|GrimHollowWB|Rime Hungers}* go last in the {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} order, and they attack T'vir unless they are targeted by a character or a character or other target is within 5 feet of them. For her turns, T'vir uses the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and keep the tree to her back unless she is told to do otherwise.

Treat T'vir like a character with regards to her {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws}. Give the characters every chance to save her, for she has an important role to play in the future of Hverfa if she lives.
If she is saved and questioned, T'vir conveys the following information.

items: The monster that attacked her and the other Hverfa warriors is a Large-sized white wolf. Its claws and bite are extremely dangerous, but its deadliest weapon is its cold breath. Upon hearing this, a character that succeeds a DC 13 Intelligence ({@skill Nature|XPHB}) check identifies the monster as a {@creature Winter Wolf|XMM}.
T'vir survived the massacre of her warband by thrusting her molten sword into the monster's muzzle. This caused the creature to flee in pain.

T'vir hasn't been alone the entire time since the battle. She was visited by an eerie woman who wore raven feathers and a black veil. T'vir thought the woman was a death spirit of Neðarheim, but she identified herself as "Svár."

Svár gave T'vir a message for Jarl Örskýrsson. The strange woman claimed Bödvar owed her his life and demanded that he travel into the woods to meet her. If the jarl did not surrender, Svár would seek justice against him and send her wolf to drag him from the village, killing anyone who tried to intervene.

Svár insists that the warriors who died be burned and their spirits freed to join their ancestors in the spirit world. This is not just the right thing to do; it'll keep them from returning as undead monsters like the {@creature Rime Hunger|GrimHollowWB|Rime Hungers}*.

entries: Unless her exhaustion is relieved, T'vir cannot be effective in battle. She and her arm need rest and careful attention or she might lose it to frostbite. She chooses to make her way back to Hverfa alone unless the party insist on keeping her close. If T'vir receives no healing, she collapses from exhaus tion and dies of her injuries shortly after she is found.

If the heroes prove to be strong and worthy of the sacrifice, she imparts her {@item T'vir's Sword|GrimHollowWB|family sword} onto one of them. The fire of the rune attached to it will be of great aid against the creatures of the forest and the beast she was hunting.
If the characters help T'vir start a bonfire to burn the bodies, continue to " Bonfire V i sitation."

entries.name: GM Advice: T'vir's Role
entries: Getting T'vir's help in dealing with the jarl and the villagers of Hverfa is key. If T'vir is with the party when they hear Svár's story, the Valikan warrior believes it and understands what must be done. She is bound by oath not to harm the jarl, but she promises to support the characters in any conflict with Bödvar by talking to anyone who might interfere with the characters. Of course, if the characters failed to rescue her, or did not bring her with them, they must succeed on a DC 12 Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check to convince her. If they have Sigurð's ring, the check automatically succeeds.

entries.name: Bonfire Visitation
entries: If the characters start a fire and begin to burn the bodies of the fallen warriors as T'vir requested, a forest fey creature takes notice and shows itself to the party.

As the fire rages, a tiny form flies down from the trees. It's clothing is made of leaves and pine needles, and its complexion is dark with white splotchy patches, like snow melting on dark wood. It's high-pitched voices pierces the cracking of the flames. "Burning people. Love that smell. Great for growing. May I join you?"

The creature is a Puck known as {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB}. It was a dryad, but it was slowly turned into its current form by the evil influence of the Winter fey court on this forest over many centuries. This is not {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB}'s home or lair, as the place belongs to {@creature Svár|GrimHollowWB} now. This makes {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB} sad and angry, and it is willing to provide some information that might help the characters defeat the Norn if the characters provide something useful.

entries.name: Negotiations
entries: {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB} begins the conversation with small talk, or the closest it can come to that: vivid and fascinated commentary on the crackling of the corpses, which types of humanoids make the best soil for plants, etc. Finally, it gets around to it's point. Something powerful rules the forest here, and {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB} doesn't like it. If the characters to a favor for {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB}, it provides valuable information about the new ruler. If the characters agree, run "Hungry Plants" and then come back here for the information {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB} provides.
Information. If the characters deal with the meyanas, {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB} provides the characters with the following information:

entries.name: Information
entries: If the characters deal with the meyanas, {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB} provides the characters with the following information:

items: A faerie creature moved into the area a long time ago in mortal years, and it wrested control of the plants and animals from {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB}.

This faerie creature is virtually indestructible while she's within the stone circle that she calls home. There's a stone within the circle that sometimes mortal or fey creatures kill things on. This ritual killing is a waste of good meat, but it seems to give the stone and the circle power.

If the magic of that stone could be turned off by someone who understands nature and magic, the faerie creature might be able to be harmed and driven off, but it is also served by both animal and plant creatures, including a big wolf that breathes cold mist.

entries: Armed with this knowledge, the characters might be ready to face the norn {@creature Svár|GrimHollowWB}.

entries.name: Hungry Plants
entries: {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB} tells the characters that some of the nearby plants have changed and no longer listen to it or obey its commands, because they serve this new faerie creature. In fact, a particularly troublesome group of plants have tried to kill and eat {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB}, making the puck quite nervous and angry. {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB} can provide the general location of these threatening plant creatures.

When the characters gets to that area, the 2 {@creature Meyana|GrimHollowWB|Meyanas}* and 2 {@creature Young Meyana|GrimHollowWB|Young Meyanas}* are hiding in the vegetation, waiting for prey to pass by. A successful DC 12 Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) check allows the characters to spot the meyanas before they attack.

Four bipedal plant creatures leap out of the vegetation. In pincer-like hands they hold clubs. Two of the creatures are over 7 feet tall, while the other two are smaller. Their torsos comprise a long, grasping frond that resembles a clam shell.

The meyanas attack until destroyed. Within their central body fronds are undigested treasures from previous victims, including 30 GP, a gold brooch worth 40 GP, and a {@item Silvered Shortsword|XDMG}.
If the characters return to {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB} after dealing with the meyanas, it provides them with the promised information about {@creature Svár|GrimHollowWB}.

entries.name: Winter Wolf Attack
entries: As the characters explore the Skuggiskogr, the {@creature Winter Wolf|XMM} may attack at any time. It tries to hide and attack using surprise, unless the party are travelling cautiously and have succeeded a DC 17 Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) group check. It attacks with a group of 6 {@creature Wolf|XMM|Wolves} to aid it. The pack is cunning and makes the best use of Pack Tactics.
The winter wolf flees combat if it starts its turn below half its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum. It also flees if more than half of the wolves in his pack are killed.
If the party pursues the winter wolf or follow its tracks in the snow, they eventually arrive at "Lunar Glade."

entries.name: Lunar Glade
entries: Following the Winter Wolf's tracks after fighting it leads to this place. It is also possible the characters discover the lunar glade while exploring the Skuggiskogr after talking to {@creature Raatvidur|GrimHollowWB}.

The canopy opens above a glade, permitting the sky to look upon the forest floor. A circle of monoliths is erected inside the clearing. Each is engraved with runes. At the center of the monument is a roughly hewn slaughter stone, its purpose betrayed by the notches in the rock and the dark stains that dribble over its edges. A raven glides from the trees and comes to rest on the shoulder of a pale woman who stands in the shadow of the tree line like a ghost.

The woman is {@creature Svár|GrimHollowWB}, a fey Norn* and weaver of fates. Her shape is spidery and stretched. Yet she moves gracefully as if buoyed by the wind. Black ravens perch on stones and branches nearby. Her white draping garments make her seem ethereal, though her eyes are as dark as a wolf's and reflect torchlight just the same.

A creature with a passive Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) score of 13 or higher notices humanoid bones poking up from beneath the snow beside the slaughter stone. A successful DC 12 Intelligence ({@skill Arcana|XPHB}) check made while inspecting the clearing deduces that this place is used for ritual sacrifices. The standing stones with runes carved upon them track the phases of the moon. Sacrifices are made at different phases. If the check is successful by 5 or more, it's apparent that the glade is used to conduct the Lunar Sacrament, a secret ritual that transforms a creature into a lycanthrope.

entries.name: Conversing with Svár
entries: It's possible for the characters to question {@creature Svár|GrimHollowWB} and learn about her vendetta against Jarl Örskýrsson. She does not hide any information, but Charisma checks may be required to convince {@creature Svár|GrimHollowWB} the characters are willing to hear her side of the story and act in a way she desires. See "Important NPCs" for information on roleplaying Svár.

If the characters wounded the winter wolf and pursued it to this location, Svár begins with an unfriendly attitude towards them. If the characters avoided injuring the wolf, or allowed it to escape, Svár thanks them and instead has a positive attitude. If the characters found this place by any other means, Svár's attitude is neutral.
During a discussion, Svár may reveal the following:

items: Twenty years ago, Svár met the sons of Örskýr av Hverfa freezing to death in Skuggiskogr. The young Valikans had come into the forest during winter to train themselves into mighty warriors who would have their own sagas written.
The norn augured that it would be their fate to die in the forest, so Svár offered them a way to survive. Sigurð refused, but Bödvar accepted.

Svár described the Lunar Sacrament and its affects, which Bödvar conducted within the lunar glade. Upon the slaughter stone he cut out his brother's heart while Sigurð was alive, then the young man ate it beneath the full moon.
Bödvar transformed into a werebear and inherited the rights to the title of Jarl of Hverfa.

Svár's help came with a condition. The surviving brother would be free for thirty more years. After his thirtieth winter, he would be forced to return to Skuggiskogr and join the winter court as Svár's retainer. The thirtieth winter since Jarl Örskýrsson made the pact has just passed.
Jarl Örskýrsson has not surrendered himself to Svár. So, the norn has conjured a winter wolf to kill Örskýrsson for oath breaking. Her wrath extends to all of Hverfa until the jarl is slain.

If the characters try to make a deal, such as saying that they can bring the jarl to her after the impending Ostoyan raid, Svár declines. She tells them that she has foreseen Örskýrsson's demise on that raid, and she demands that he be brought to her immediately.

entries: If Svár is friendly towards the characters, she may offer an alternative solution to sending the winter wolf to destroy Hverfa. If the party are willing to slay Jarl Örskýrsson themselves, or bring him into the forest, Svár promises to bring the winter wolf to heel and spare the village. If the characters agree, the norn casts a charm that makes their weapons magical for 24 hours. She also gives them an emerald ring that belonged to Sigurð as proof of Jarl Örskýrsson's deed. Magical auguries on the ring would show how Sigurð perished at his brother's hand.

entries.name: Fighting Svár
entries: If Svár is attacked, the {@creature Winter Wolf|XMM} that is lurking nearby immediately comes to the aid of the Norn\*. If the wolf has taken damage from previous encounters, that damage has been healed by Svár. She can also conjure 4 {@creature Awakened Shrub|XMM|Awakened Shrubs} as a Lair Action while inside the circle. The shrubs appear immediately in an empty space within 60 feet of Svár and act on her initiative.

entries.name: The Circle's Magic
entries: While within the circle of standing stones, Svár is immune to all damage. However, that magical protection can be momentarily suppressed, removing that Immunity. If a character uses a Magic action while standing next to the slaughter stone and succeeds on a DC 15 Intelligence ({@skill Arcana|XPHB} or {@skill Nature|XPHB}) check, Svár's Immunity to damage is removed until the end of that character's next turn.

If Svár is reduced lower than half her {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum, she teleports away from the combat and appears elsewhere in the forest at the start of her next turn. Her last words to the party are a threat that she will return next winter with even greater minions. The {@creature Winter Wolf|XMM} and {@creature Awakened Shrub|XMM|Awakened Shrubs} remain and fight until slain.

data.name: Part 3: Justice Or Judgement
entries: The players should be armed with the knowledge of what really happened thirty years ago in the forest. How they choose to proceed with that knowledge determines what happens in the rest of the adventure.

entries.name: Return to the Longhouse
entries: When the players return to the Hverfa longhouse, it is less crowded than earlier. However, some folks from the village still gather to witness what occurs.

If the characters return with the winter wolf's head, Jarl Örskýrsson rewards them as promised. If the jarl is warned of Svár's parting threat after her defeat, he invites them to join Hverfa for a year and help the village prepare for next winter's attack. He also invites them to join in the upcoming raids against coastal Ostoyan villages.

If the characters instead return to expose Jarl Örskýrsson's secret, he quickly becomes antagonistic and denies their accusations. The jarl demands the characters cease speaking or suffer his wrath. If the characters attack the jarl or present Sigurð's emerald ring as proof of his misdeed, the jarl changes into his bear form and fights back.

In combat the Jarl is a Werebear (see {@creature Bödvar Örskýrsson|GrimHollowWB}) who prefers to make claw attacks. The jarl is aided in combat by 9 {@creature Valikan Berserker|GrimHollowWB|Valikan Berserkers}. That number is reduced by 2 if the characters show the ring they received from Svár as proof of the treachery. If the characters saved T'vir and got her back to the village safely, the number of berserkers is reduced by another 2. Another 2 can be removed if the characters succeed on a DC 13 Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check to convince them of the jarl's treachery.

entries.name: GM Advice: Final Encounter
entries: What's important to roleplaying Jarl Örskýrsson in this final encounter is understanding his motivations and the extreme importance Valikans place on oaths, glory, loyalty, kinship, and community. Slaying his own brother to usurp Hverfa's jarldom was not Bödvar's only crime. The jarl has also now broken an oath to Svár and lied to the village about the fey bargain. He has risked the lives in his community to protect himself as a coward. The stakes for keeping the truth hidden are incredibly high for the jarl. Örskýrsson denies every accusation fervently unless he is cornered by proof of his misdeeds, such as Sigurð's ring. If the party have no proof, the jarl likely attacks simply to silence them.

data.name: Conclusion
entries: The fate of Hverfa depends on many actions and decisions made by the characters' during the adventure. The status quo may be returned to the village if the winter wolf was slain and the jarl's secret was kept hidden. Or Hverfa could have entirely new leadership. The following are suggested outcomes to the characters' actions, though they are far from only endings this story could have.

items: If Jarl Örskýrsson survived the adventure, he likely continues to rule over Hverfa. If his secret is revealed but he is not slain, the disgraced jarl may be exiled instead. His animosity towards the characters may one day lead him to seek vengeance.

If Svár is not slain or otherwise banished, she continues to make bargains in exchange for boons with the folk of Hverfa. She tries to exert her influence over a new jarl or perhaps the characters themselves.

T'vir Setstaðr is highly respected in the village. Her renown grows if she survived the winter wolf's assualt in the Skuggiskogr, even more so if she holds her family's magic sword. T'vir would make a fitting successor to Jarl Örskýrsson and rewards the party for aiding Hverfa.

If Jarl Örskýrsson is slain and T'vir did not survive, the characters' themselves could take rulership of Hverfa if they had the respect of key community members and are members of the community already. Hverfa could become like a bastion for them to return to and protect in winter, whilst they explore the rest of Etharis during the warmer months.

item.name: Porridge of Healing
entries: This foul-tasting porridge is a magic item. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can eat it or administer it to another creature within 5 feet of yourself. The creature that eats the porridge regains {@dice 2d4 + 2} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

item.name: T'vir's Sword
entries: T'vir's ancestral sword is etched with magical runes along the middle of the blade. These symbols heat the sword as if it is held within a furnace. The weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d6} Fire damage to any target it hits. When you take Cold damage, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to give yourself and up to 6 other creatures that you can see within 30 feet of you {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the triggering Cold damage.

trait.name: Keen Smell
entries: Bödvar has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks that rely on smell.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: In bear form, Bödvar makes two Rend attacks. In humanoid form, he makes two Greataxe attacks. In hybrid form, he can attack like a bear or a humanoid. He can replace one attack with a Bite attack.

action.name: Bite (Bear or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft. {@h}15 ({@damage 2d10 + 4}) Piercing damage. If the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 14}. {@actSaveFail} The target is cursed. If the cursed target drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it instead becomes a {@creature Werebear|XMM} under the DM's control and has 10 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. {@actSaveSuccess} The target is immune to this werebear's curse for 24 hours.

action.name: Greataxe (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft.. {@h}10 ({@damage 1d12 + 4}) Slashing damage.

action.name: Rend (Bear or Hybrid Form Only)
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 7} to hit, reach 5 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 2d8 + 4}) Slashing damage.

bonus.name: Shape-Shift
entries: Bödvar shape-shifts into a Large bear-humanoid hybrid form or a Large bear, or he returns to his true humanoid form. His game statistics, other than his size, are the same in each form. Any equipment he is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The meyana makes one Bite attack and one Greatclub attack.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage plus 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Acid damage, or 10 ({@damage 4d4}) Acid damage if the target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the meyana. If the target is Medium or smaller, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 12}). Until the grapple ends, the meyana can't Bite another target.

action.name: Greatclub
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. {@h}6 ({@damage 1d8 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 3}, reach 5 ft. {@h}3 ({@damage 1d4 + 1}) Bludgeoning damage plus 3 ({@damage 1d6}) Acid damage, or 5 ({@damage 2d4}) Acid damage if the target is {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the meyana. If the target is Small or smaller, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 11}). Until the grapple ends, the meyana can't Bite another target.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
headerEntries: The norn casts one of the following spells, using Charisma as its spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}):

trait.name: Strands of Fate
entries: When the norn rolls for {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, they roll {@dice 3d20} and note down each result. When a creature the norn can see makes a {@dice D20} Test, the norn can choose to replace the number the character rolled on the dice with one of these results instead. Once a result has been used in this way, it is discarded.

trait.name: Nimble Movement
entries: The norn never provokes Opportunity Attacks.

action.name: Wilting Touch
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 5}, reach 5 ft. {@h}13 ({@damage 3d6 + 3}) Necrotic damage.

bonus.name: Bolstering Spirit
entries: The norn chooses a creature it can see within 60 feet of itself. That creature regains 8 ({@dice 2d4 + 3}) {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

reaction.name: Swift Escape
entries: {@actTrigger} The norn is hit by a melee attack.   Response: The attack misses and the norn moves up to its {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}.

spellcasting.name: Innate Spellcasting
headerEntries: The puck casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save {@dc 13}):

trait.name: Fey Evasion
entries: If the puck is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a saving throw to take only half damage, the puck instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw, and only half damage if it fails.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The puck has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Plant Bond
entries: If the puck remains within 20 feet of a Huge or smaller plant for 8 hours, it can use special dust to form a bond with the plant, granting it sapience as if by the {@spell Awaken|XPHB} spell. The target gains the ability to understand Sylvan and becomes a puck version of an animated plant, such as a puck tree or puck shrub.

The puck can have only one plant {@condition Charmed|XPHB} in this way at a time. The condition ends if the puck harms the plant, and the plant can make a {@dc 10} Wisdom saving throw to end the condition if a request from the puck guarantees the plant's destruction. Plants freed from the charm leave the puck's grove, becoming inanimate again after 24 hours. Otherwise, the plant is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by the puck for 30 days, after which the plant becomes the puck's willing servant.

The puck is emotionally bonded with each plant it has animated, {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, and enthralled. Provided such a plant is within 1 mile, the puck is aware when the plant takes damage and knows its location. If such a plant is destroyed, the puck has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn as it grieves.

action.name: Superior Invisibility
entries: The puck magically becomes {@condition Invisible|XPHB} until its {@status Concentration|XPHB} ends. Any equipment the puck wears or carries is {@condition Invisible|XPHB} with it.

bonus.name: Plant Manifestation
entries: The puck causes a plantlike duplicate of itself to emerge from a Plant within 120 feet that is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or enthralled by it. The puck can act through this duplicate until the end of its turn, at which point the duplicate disappears.

reaction.name: Uncanny Dodge
entries: {@actTrigger} A creature the puck can see hits it with an attack.   Response: The puck halves the damage it takes from the attack.

legendary.name: Illusionary Menace
entries: Six images of the puck appear in multiple locations, each moving and speaking independently during the puck's turn. At the start and end of the puck's turn, and one time during the puck's movement, it can teleport to the location of one of these images. The images remain until the next round.

legendary.name: Hostile Nature
entries: Grasping vines and roots manifest in the lair. Strength or {@actSave dex} {@dc 10}, each creature hostile to the puck in the lair. {@actSaveFail} The target is moved up to 20 feet to another unoccupied space in the lair and gains the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

legendary.name: Poisonous Plants
entries: Inanimate plants in the lair twist and sprout thorns. The area becomes {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} to creatures hostile to the puck until the next round, and are subject to the following effect. {@actSave con} {@dc 10} when a creature moves more than half its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} in the area. {@actSaveFail} The creature gains the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. {@actSaveSuccess} the creature is Immune to the Poisonous Plants effect for 24 hours.

monster.name: Rime Hunger
languages: understands the languages it knew in life

trait.name: Fed Fortitude
entries: For 1 minute after the rime hunger regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, when damage reduces the rime hunger to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it can make a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 5} plus the damage taken), unless the damage is Fire, Radiant, or from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. On a successful save, the rime hunger drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

action.name: Slam
entries: {@atkr m} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft.   {@h}9 ({@damage 2d6 + 2}) Bludgeoning damage, and if the target is Medium or smaller, it has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition (escape {@dc 12}). The rime hunger can only grapple one target at a time.

action.name: Sapping Grasp
entries: {@actSave con} {@dc 13}, one creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by the rime hunger.   Failure: 9 ({@damage 2d8}) Cold damage, and the rime hunger regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the Cold damage dealt.   Success: Half damage only.

trait.name: Berserker Endurance (Recharge after a Short or Long Rest)
entries: If the berserker takes 10 damage or less that would reduce it to 0 hit points, it is reduced to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The berserker makes two handaxe attacks.

action.name: Handaxe
entries: {@atkr m,r} {@hit 4}, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target.   {@h}5 ({@damage 1d6 + 2}) Slashing damage.

bonus.name: Aggressive
entries: The berserker moves up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} toward a hostile creature it can see.

monsterFluff.name: Bödvar Örskýrsson
entries: {@i I will be jarl until my last breath!}

entries.name: Stubborn as a Bear
entries: Jarl Örskýrsson's lycanthropic curse is a wellkept secret. Not even his closest berserkers realize this is why no blade seems to touch him in battle.

entries.name: Battle Tactics
entries: Jarl Örskýrsson is used to fighting with a {@item Greataxe|XPHB} inside or behind a shield wall, where his powerful swings eviscerate any foe within reach. Örskýrsson will prefer to gang up on single targets with his berserkers, fighting as a grouped unit. However, if Örskýrsson is provoked into a rage (likely by the truth of his brother's fate being exposed) he transforms into his bear form and attacks whoever is the closest within reach. He uses his claws and spreads his attacks to try and inflict as much carnage as possible. He never uses his bite attack (or risks creating another werebear) unless he is consumed by his lycanthropic instincts.

monsterFluff.name: Meyana
entries: {@i "Some people eat only plants. A few plants are happy to return the favor."}
Meyanas are bipedal plant creatures that walk on leglike roots, and they can hold and utilize tools in appendages resembling arms. These appendages end in dangerous pincers.

monsterFluff.name: Young Meyana
entries: {@i "Some people eat only plants. A few plants are happy to return the favor."}
Meyanas are bipedal plant creatures that walk on leglike roots, and they can hold and utilize tools in appendages resembling arms. These appendages end in dangerous pincers.

monsterFluff.name: Raatvidur
entries: Raatvidur was once a dryad that cared for certain trees in the Skuggiskogr, but the corrupting magic of the Winter fey court changed it into a puck. It now travels the forest looking to grow new trees in ground infused with the corpses of fleshy creatures. It wants its former domain back, but Svár now rules this part of the forest instead. It is curious and talkative in a horrifying way.

entries.name: Puck
entries: {@i "I've heard pixie dust is beautiful, magical stuff. The dust I saw was definitely magical, but it wasn't beautiful."}

A puck is a pixie whose hopes and dreams, loves and playfulness, have been erased by mistreatment, betrayal, or loss. Once a beautiful and merry being, a puck is now a sad, twisted monster. Once the transformation happens, the pixie's dust dissipates. Without pixie dust, a pixie can't fly, can't use its magic, and can't turn {@condition invisible}. They lose their ability to commune with nature.

monsterFluff.name: Rime Hunger
entries: Rarely straying far from where they died and never willingly wandering into areas of warmth and sunlight, rime hungers are frost-covered, sickly blue corpses that haunt frozen regions in continuous search for victims.

monsterFluff.name: Valikan Berserker
entries: {@i We die with the taste of our enemies blood on our lips!}

entries.name: Feared and Hated
entries: Valikan berserkers are feared and hated in equal measure. They are sometimes equally unpopular among their fellow clanfolk as much as those from the coastal villages they raid. They are rough, violent, and cruel by habit. They respect no one except their fellows and those who lead them. It takes a fierce but tempered leader to train a warband of berserkers, and keep them from becoming ruffians in their own communities.

entries.name: Theatrical Violence
entries: Berserkers are trained to draw strength from their trauma, rage, and bestial instincts. Before battle they spend time together beating their chests, gnashing their teeth, and howling at the sky. They wear the pelts of animals they wish to embody, be that bear, wolf, or boar. Yet unlike true lycanthropes, this display of beastliness is spiritual and not physical.

monsterFluff.name: Svár
entries: Svár is an ancient fey queen of the winter court. Her domain once stretched much further than the Skuggiskogr. She has, at different times, been an aid and an adversary to the Valikans making their homes on the forest's edge. Though her whims seem capricious to the clanfolk who know her (usually by different names in different villages), earning her favor usually depends on whether she feels respected and appreciated.

As a norn, Svár is a weaver of fates. She provides gifts that allow her beneficiaries to seize a bright destiny, though she never offers a thing without expecting recompense. Though her wrath can be overbearing when she feels betrayed, Svár is not cruel—she's simply contractual. She believes any suffering experienced by mortals is the result of their own choices, never her undue malice.

entries.name: Norn
entries: {@i A person's fate is not their own. We're all playthings for greater beings.}

entries.name: Fate Weavers
entries: Norns are powerful fey who pluck the strands of fate to manipulate destiny. They act as advisors to fey kings and queens, though often hatch their own schemes as well. They use their skills in divination to manipulate others and grow their influence, whether within they fey courts or a region they've claimed as their own.

entries.name: Hag Kin
entries: Norns are frequently mistaken for hags, though hag is really a mortal word for a variety of differing fey kin. Though norns are manipulative, use animal familiars and divine futures, they are not always malicious. They also don't live in huts or stir cauldrons, and don't need a broomstick to fly.

entries.name: Ethereal Spirits
entries: A norn's physical appearance is unimposing, though very strange. They are shaped like humanoids draped in garments of white, red or brown. Yet they move swiftly and ethereally, as if their body had no physical weight. Stories of ghosts whispering ill portents are as likely to have originated from encountering a norn.
